plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Instrument refresh latency against the architecture budgets
node.summary: Add the missing hot-path timing breakdowns and budget scoreboards so refresh, commit, publish, settle, and checkpoint costs are attributable from the live daemon log.

acceptance.label: The daemon exposes queue wait, lock hold, prepare, delta construction, commit, publish, and settle timing fields with enough detail to explain the current missing 'other' bucket.
A repeatable benchmark or log-extraction workflow reports p50 and p95 for tiny-edit publish latency, queue wait, direct dependent settle, and checkpoint lag.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Define the incremental delta-commit contract for refresh
node.summary: Specify the exact boundary between parallel file-local preparation, tiny authoritative commit, and parallel settle so ordinary edits stop flowing through reconstructive WorkspaceIndexer refresh.

acceptance.label: The design names the committed delta payload, file-facts substrate, domain freshness semantics, and what work is permitted inside the serialized commit.
The design explicitly removes reconstructive refresh from the normal edit path and identifies the residual recovery-only rebuild path.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Move file-local delta construction out of the serialized refresh path
node.summary: Build immutable candidate deltas in parallel from changed files and invalidation inputs, instead of mutating authoritative graph/history/outcome/projection state file-by-file inside the current refresh loop.

acceptance.label: File-local parse and candidate delta construction run off the authoritative state and can execute in parallel for multi-file refreshes.
The current serial apply_parsed loop no longer owns parse-time history, co-change, outcome, and projection mutation work.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Reduce commit to authoritative apply, journal append, and generation publish
node.summary: Make the serialized commit kernel only apply prebuilt deltas to mutable runtime state, append the committed batch, and publish the next immutable generation with explicit domain freshness.

acceptance.label: The commit path excludes broad parsing, rebuild, projection mutation, memory reanchor, and checkpoint work.
Generation publication exposes honest freshness for any domains left to settle after commit.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Move derived projections, memory reanchor, and coordination reloads fully behind settle
node.summary: Push non-authoritative downstream work behind coalesced settle lanes and surface pending or stale domain states rather than doing hidden work on the publish path.

acceptance.label: Projection reload, memory reanchor, and coordination refresh run as explicit settle work with target-generation cancellation or coalescing.
Queries and runtime status expose pending or stale domains instead of implying fully current derived state after the fast publish path.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Separate hot commit persistence from asynchronous materialization and remove SQLite contention
node.summary: Keep the authoritative commit journal and minimal runtime state durable on the hot path, but push derived snapshots and checkpoint materialization behind low-contention workers that stop fighting SQLite locks.

acceptance.label: The hot commit transaction excludes derived graph or projection materialization work not needed for correctness.
Nominal daemon usage no longer produces recurring checkpoint materializer 'database is locked' warnings.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Tighten coalescing, stale-work cancellation, and invalidation precision for repeated edits
node.summary: Reduce repeated work under edit storms by coalescing path requests to the latest revision, cancelling stale prepare or settle work aggressively, and shrinking dependency fanout for ordinary small edits.

acceptance.label: Repeated edits to the same paths cancel or supersede stale prepare and settle work instead of replaying obsolete refreshes.
Small edits reduce dependency refresh scope and direct dependent work compared with the current reconstructive path.

plan.title: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.
plan.goal: Hit the refresh latency targets in docs/RUNTIME_REWRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md by replacing reconstructive workspace refresh with a true parallel-prepare / tiny-commit / parallel-settle runtime path, reducing hot-path persistence and lock contention, and validating the resulting daemon behavior against explicit p50/p95 budgets.

node.title: Validate refresh latency against the document budgets and publish residual gaps
node.summary: Use live daemon evidence and reproducible workloads to confirm whether the rewritten path hits the architecture budgets, and publish any remaining misses with attributed causes rather than hand-waving them away.

acceptance.label: The repo has reproducible measurements for tiny-edit publish latency, queue wait, direct dependent settle latency, and checkpoint lag under nominal load.
If any budget is still missed, the remaining gap is attributed to named subsystems with follow-up work, not a generic 'needs tuning' conclusion.
